description: §14.8.9 egress redaction — the RUNTIME proof that a CTE over a protect= table ships NO data to the client. A client button calls the server fn getUser (a `WITH t AS (SELECT * FROM users ...) SELECT * FROM t` CTE); the E-ADAPTER server-eval path runs the REAL emitted route handler, so `_scrml_protect_redact` executes at the egress sink. Because the CTE row has no statically-resolvable origin it is tagged `"*"` (strip-all) and redact returns `{}` per row — the client-bound cell @user observes an EMPTY object: neither passwordHash NOR any other column crosses. REGRESSION GUARD for the S249 fail-open where the whole (protected) row shipped verbatim. Compile half pinned: I-PROTECT-STRIP-001 (info) names the wholesale strip.
rationale: §14.8.9 (SPEC.md I-PROTECT-STRIP-001 row) + §14.8.7 CTE degradation: an unresolvable-origin row is stripped WHOLESALE at egress, never accept-unknown. The runtime proof is that the client cell holds `{}` — passwordHash never crosses (and neither does id/name, the fail-closed posture). auth="none" declassifies the AUTH scope (dodging the auth-path CSRF gate) while the §14.8.9 column-redaction floor stays engaged (column-redaction ⟂ auth-scope, SPEC.md §52).
